verdict: 2026-07-08 16:32:57 PDT — The role source is the right one: `getUserContext().role` is the privilege signal, while `participantsAvailable()` should remain a participant-list availability signal because it conflates role with scope/config/fetch failures. The pure `lib/role.js` helper, booting through the existing `booted` object, and a tiny count-display helper all fit the repo’s conventions and do not need a new dependency. I would not approve the sketch as-is because it misses one existing App-level availability gate and the shrink plan risks deleting the server-storage disclosure.

findings.title: Simple mode still inherits the global participants-unavailable blocker
findings.locus: Design sketch — Role flow / Empty-prompt count; current client/src/App.jsx:302
findings.claim: The sketch only routes `participantsAvailable` into `SimpleCostPanel`, but the current App renders the “Participants unavailable” state before checking session or cost model. After this design, non-host Simple mode should calculate from manual attendee count even when the participant list is unavailable; leaving that global gate means the primary readout can still say it cannot calculate and suppress `SharedCostScreen`, violating the Simple-first and empty-prompt acceptance criteria.
findings.alternative: Derive an effective model in `App`, e.g. `effectiveCostModel = canPerParticipant ? config.costModel : 'simple'`, then derive `participantListRequired = effectiveCostModel !== 'simple'`. Use that effective model for totals/summary/readout and show the unavailable block only when `participantListRequired && !participantsAvailable`. Keep passing `participantsAvailable` to the Simple count helper only to decide whether the field is blank/prompted.
findings.win: Centralizes the role lock and list-availability invariant, removes the false “cannot calculate” path for non-hosts, and makes the $0-until-filled Simple behavior testable from one predicate.

findings.title: Panel shrink can remove the private-data disclosure
findings.claim: The shrink plan says to delete secondary helper paragraphs, but the secondary paragraph in the private per-person values panel is the disclosure that values are saved server-side, encrypted but operator-decryptable, and included in export/delete. Mechanically deleting secondary paragraphs would be a privacy/permission regression for hosts/co-hosts who still use per-participant mode.
findings.alternative: Keep the one-line-helper rule, but explicitly preserve that disclosure as a concise single note in the private-values panel instead of deleting it.
findings.win: Satisfies the panel-shrink criterion without removing the app’s informed-consent notice for persisted per-person data.
